query: How much does parking cost at the Big E?

documents.text: Eastern States Exposition - The Big E On-site parking for cars/trucks is available for $10 (cash only) at Gate 9. Motorcycles can park for $5 (cash only) at Gate 9. Bicycles can park for FREE at 915 ... Motorcycles can park for $5 (cash only) at Gate 9. Bicycles can park for FREE at 915 Memorial Avenue. The Big E provides 600 handicapped accessible parking spaces at Gate 9 on a first-come, first-served basis. The Gate 9A area is available for drop-off/pick-up of passengers and has a large canvas tent with benches for patrons. Take a shuttle or train to The Big E . Wheelchairs and a limited number of oversized wheelchairs are $25 per day. Electric scooters are $70 per day and may not be available on extremely wet days. Wheelchairs and scooters from Scootaround can rented on the grounds during the Fair at Gates 4, 9A and at the Transportation Center. Strollers ($11), double strollers and wagons ($17) can also be rented at Gates 4 and 9A. If you would like to rent a scooter or wheelchair in advance you will need to make a reservation 24 hours prior to the day you plan to attend the Fair. Rent a scooter or wheelchair online. All reservations made in advance require a credit card payment to reserve over the phone or online. Walk up rentals have the option to pay with cash or credit. Parking spaces at Gate 9 are available on a first come, first served basis for visitors with self-contained and pop-up campers. Spaces per day are $20 and additional cars or awnings are $10 per space/per day. Visitors with tents are $20 per space/per day with car included. Portable restroom facilities are nearby though no other amenities will be provided during Fairtime. ATM's can be found along the Avenue of States and inside the Coliseum, Young Building, Better Living Center, Mallary Complex, Food Court, Transportation Center and the Midway.

The Big E 2022: How much is parking? Where can I park? - MassLive Parking outside The Big E ranged from $15 to $20 at nearby lots. Parking at The Big E, however, only costs $10 at gate 9. But price isn’t the only factor when making a parking decision at the third largest fair in North American. Traffic can get backed up when driving into and leaving gate 9. Some of the signs outside The Big E parking lots advertise for quicker times related to coming and going. Many of them are also only a three to five minute walk to gate 9. The Big E parking is also cash only, while some of the outside parking lots take Venmo. The Big E warns there are no ATMs near the parking lot. Parking at The Big E 2023: Where to park at America’s 3rd-largest fair With The Big E’s attendance records shattered last season at 177,789 people in a single day, it’s a smart idea to plan ahead for parking at America’s third-largest fair as New England-fanatics begin their annual descent on West Springfield this year from Sept. 15 until Oct. 1. After buying your tickets ahead of time, the fair’s public parking offers the cheapest available in the area for cars, vans and trucks on the fairgrounds at Gate 9, located at 875 Memorial Ave. in West Springfield. The ground’s parking is cash only and costs $10. The Big E warns there are no ATMs close to Gate 9, so plan accordingly. Motorcycles can park for $5. A paved handicapped parking area is located inside the Gate 9 parking area at Gate 9A, with 580 spaces on a first-come, first-served basis available. There is additional handicapped parking at a partially paved 1,000-spot plot near Gate 9A, and people with disabilities, the elderly or others requiring assistance can also wait at a covered drop-off area at the 9A Gate. But for those not keen on the backed-up traffic perpetually surrounding Gate 9, private lots are available throughout the neighborhood and are not affiliated with The Big E. The property owners set their own parking fees, and vary depending on the day and the distance from the fair. Many of the lots are a three- to five-minute walk to Gate 9, and can often provide a quicker arrival and escape at the end of the day. Don’t want to drive? A free shuttle is offered Saturdays and Sundays from Union Station in downtown Springfield. Find the bus schedule here. The fan-favorite fair in West Springfield brings together all six New England states for two weeks of fun, food, and music! Here’s everything you need to know for this year’s state fair: What is the cost of admission? The Big E offers a full season pass that you could purchase, which costs $70 for adults, and $35 for children ages 6-12 costs. If you purchase tickets at the gate, it costs $20 for adults, $12 for children 6-12, and children under 5 get in for free. To purchase tickets online, visit the link here. What time do the gates and buildings open? The gates open at 8:00 a.m. every day. The Big E Bakery is open from 9:00 a.m. until 10:30 p.m. The Buildings and Craft Common is open from 10:00 a.m. to 10:00 p.m. The Avenue of States and Storrowton Village Museum & Shops is open from 10:00 a.m. to 9:00 p.m. On-site parking is available for $10 (cash only) in Lot 9. Parking & Directions - Eastern States Exposition Year-round event parking is $5 (cash only) for non-fair shows held at Eastern States Exposition. Learn more about Fairtime parking for all vehicles. Take the Orient Point Ferry to New London, CT or the Port Jefferson Ferry to Bridgeport. From New London, follow I-95 South and from Bridgeport, follow I-95 North to New Haven and follow above directions from Connecticut and Points South. If you are coming to the Executive Offices of Eastern States Exposition, use 1305 Memorial Avenue, West Springfield, Mass., as your destination address (coordinates: 42 °05’34.63″N – 72 °37’09.46″W – Elev. 57′). Please note: This location has parking for Administration Building only – does not allow admission onto fairgrounds. Parking Year-round event parking is $5 (cash only) for non-fair shows held at Eastern States Exposition. Learn more about Fairtime parking for all vehicles (including campers) on TheBigE.com. Vehicle and pedestrian traffic is through Gates 9 and Gate 1 or Gate 2 when open. There is no pedestrian access at Gates 4 and 5 during non-fair time. Drop offs and ride shares should proceed to Gate 9. Please note: On-street parking on Memorial Avenue and adjacent streets or commercial lots during year-round events is strongly discouraged, as this impacts our neighboring residents and businesses. Be warned: Parking is cash only, and there are no ATMs near ... 5, or you can purchase a specific number of ride tickets, which can be redeemed as you board the ride. The cheapest rides cost nine tickets, while the most expensive — the giant ferris wheel — costs 21 tickets. Here’s how much it costs to purchase ride tickets. 1 Ticket – $.50 20 Tickets – $10 45 Tickets – $20 65 Tickets – $30 140 Tickets – $60 240 Tickets – $98 And here is the full list of carnival rides at The Big E, as well as ticket costs and height requirements. The Big E: 2024: Hours, Parking and Transportation When does The Big E 2024 open? Visitors to The Big E 2024 have plenty of flexibility when it comes to planning their day. Gates to the fairgrounds open at 8 a.m., with most shops and attractions opening at 10 a.m. and areas generally closing down for the night sometime between 9 p.m. and 11 p.m. Parking and Transportation The Big E is a short drive from several major highways: I-91 for guests hailing from points North and South, and I-90 for visitors traveling from the East and West. Parking access for cars, vans, and trucks is at Gate 9, and costs $10, while motorcycles cost $5. Be warned: Parking is cash only, and there are no ATMs near Gate 9. If you’re dropping off or picking up guests, you can utilize Gate 9A. If you’d prefer to avoid the long waits at the parking lots, you can take a free shuttle from Union Station in Springfield. Music at The Big E 2024 Ludacris performed during halftime of the NFL Super Bowl 58 football game Sunday, Feb. 11, 2024, in Las Vegas. The rapper will take the stage at The Big E this fall.

baseline.final_answer: Parking at The Big E costs $10 for cars, trucks, and vans, and $5 for motorcycles. Payment is cash only, and there are no ATMs near the parking lot at Gate 9.

evaluation.feedback: The answer contains factual claims about parking costs and payment methods, but it lacks citations. Therefore, it is not grounded in the provided documents.
